locked_decisions: rudironsoni/ixland is the center of gravity [1][2][3]
rudironsoni/ish:feat/aarch64-migration is the donor repository for the Linux compatibility lane [4][5][6]
ixland-system remains the canonical semantic kernel owner [2][3]
ixland-system/compatibility/linux/ becomes the bounded Linux lane
/proc, /dev, PTY/TTY, task, signal, sockets, poll, and VFS remain canonical ixland-system ownership, with Linux-specific projections and translations layered on top [2][3][7][8]
native ixland remains the long-term primary target, WASIX remains the portable middle lane, and iSH-derived Linux remains the fallback floor [INFERENCE]
Apple platform policy, packaging, sandbox, file-access, and lifecycle constraints remain hard architectural inputs, not late product details [9][10][11]

stabilization_gates.name: Donor freeze
criteria: pick one donor SHA and stop importing from a moving branch

stabilization_gates.name: Host-boundary linked
criteria: code is reachable only through IxlandLinuxCompatibilityHost

stabilization_gates.name: Canonical semantic ownership established
criteria: ixland-system owns the shared semantic concern
Linux lane only provides translation/projection/view logic

stabilization_gates.name: Session and workspace integration
criteria: Linux lane can attach to ixland sessions, workspaces, distro instances, and task graph

stabilization_gates.name: Shell-readiness quality gate
criteria: runtime reaches a stable prompt or equivalent shell-readiness checkpoint
only then may this be product-exposed

rows.rationale: Keep donor runtime facts, discard donor product framing. [4]

rows.source: profiling docs / Meson / Just docs [INFERENCE from donor README]
rows.rationale: Keep profiling/debug value, but do not preserve donor build story as monorepo truth. [4]

destination: no direct destination, reflected into root CMake targets
rows.rationale: Useful as target inventory only. ixland build truth is CMake, not donor SwiftPM. [1][4]

destination: no long-term destination, temporary settings reference only
rows.rationale: Do not preserve a second build authority inside the monorepo. [1]
Preserve semantic tracing and instrumentation runtime, but normalize into ixland naming and ownership. [5]
Bridge logic is useful, but must become lane-neutral where possible. [5]
The donor tracing API already expresses the right semantic direction. [5]
It is a donor umbrella header, not a valid future public API. [6]
Pure Linux guest execution machinery. This belongs fully inside the compatibility lane. [6][7]
Shared guest emulation support for the Linux lane only. [6][7]
TCTI is mechanism-specific runtime machinery, not a semantic-kernel concern. [4][6]
Same rationale, AArch64-specific implementation core. [4][6]
Linux guest architecture-specific ABI and runtime glue should stay inside the lane. [6][7]
This is Linux syscall dispatch, but must be mediated through host-boundary ownership. [6][7]
Runtime-local helper, low-risk import if kept inside the lane. [6]
Linux ELF metadata is lane-specific. [7]
Linux ABI surface belongs to the lane, not to native ixland libc. [7]
This file mixes Linux-specific ELF/interpreter work with startup glue that must be mediated by ixland-system. [7]
The donor file contains both reusable semantic-kernel logic and Linux-guest runtime ownership. It must not be mirrored whole. [8]
Signals are a semantic-kernel concern. Linux numbering, masks, and guest-frame behavior are lane-specific. [8]
Guest-arch signal-frame details are lane-specific. [8]
FD ownership must be canonical in ixland-system, not duplicated under Linux compatibility. [2][3][7]
Path/mount/inode/stat are core semantic-kernel concerns. [2][3]
Pipe semantics are shared across lanes. [2][3]
Polling is a canonical kernel surface and must not be lane-owned. [2][3]
PTY/TTY ownership must remain canonical. Linux lane later provides guest-facing translation only. [2][3][12]
/dev is a canonical kernel surface; Linux lane only owns numbering/naming/ioctl translation. [2][3][12]
/proc belongs to ixland-system. Linux lane supplies guest-facing renderers and Linux field formatting. [2][3][10][11]
Socket ownership belongs centrally; Linux lane may still need guest-specific mappings. [2][3]

rows.source: Sources/IXLandLinuxRuntime/fs/fake*, real.*, tmp.*, mem.*, adhoc.*, dir.*, generic.*
rows.rationale: Donor storage code reflects iSH needs. ixland workspaces must be shaped around Files.app, security-scoped access, and explicit import/export. [9]
Canonical time ownership is required for native, WASIX, and Linux lanes. [INFERENCE]
Shared semantic kernel concern. [INFERENCE]
Shared kernel concern, later translated into Linux-specific views as needed. [INFERENCE]
These are part of the Linux-like semantic target needed by native ixland too, not just by the compatibility lane. [INFERENCE]

destination: split into canonical VFS/process ownership and Linux guest translation
rows.rationale: Cross-cutting donor file; do not preserve as a monolithic owner. [INFERENCE]
Darwin host integration should be centralized where possible, not duplicated. [2][3]
Utility code is low-risk but should not define architecture by convenience. [INFERENCE]
The donor app shell is too terminal-controller-centric and bootstraps the runtime directly. That must not become ixland architecture. [6]

destination: no direct import; derive only minimal terminal-surface behaviors if needed
rows.rationale: UI must consume IxlandSession, not directly call init-child/exec/task-start flows. [6]
Useful for debug reduction only. Must not become product runtime-mode architecture. [6]
Preserve runtime-level truth under quarantine. [2]
Needed for stabilization independent of ixland app. [2]
Useful once host boundary and session integration exist. [2]

destination: none as truth; rewrite as ixland session/workspace/product tests
rows.rationale: Product tests must reflect ixland architecture, not donor terminal-controller architecture. [INFERENCE]
